title: Tinplate Merchant Systems — Company Profile
body:
FICTIONAL COMPANY.

Tinplate Merchant Systems is a mid-market fintech company founded in 2017, headquartered in Austin TX, ~1,840 employees. Financial technology sector, focused on payments for merchants processing $500k–$50M annual volume.

FINANCIALS (as of Q4 2025):
- TTM revenue: $412M (+34% YoY)
- Q4 2025 revenue: $118M (beat consensus of $109M)
- Gross margin: 61% (up 7 points YoY)
- Net income TTM: -$28M (still burning cash)
- Cash on hand: $195M
- Last raise: Series D, $180M, Nov 2024 (lead: Shalemark Venture Partners)
- Guidance: operating breakeven in H2 2026

RECENT MOVES:
- Feb 2026: Launched 'Tinplate Capital' embedded lending product for merchants >$50k/month volume
- Jan 2026: Hired Dana Okafor (ex-Ironvane Freight Systems CPO) as Head of Platform
- Jan 2026: 47-minute checkout outage affecting ~12% of merchants (DB failover issue, postmortem published)
- Dec 2025: Expanded to Canada and UK — first international markets

COMPETITIVE: ~4.2% market share. Direct competitors include Bucklefern Commerce Holdings, Wharfton Payment Rails, Bronzefathom Transaction Works. Differentiates on dev experience and embedded financial products. Weaker than competitors on in-person/POS.

title: Bucklefern Commerce Holdings — Company Profile
body:
FICTIONAL COMPANY.

Bucklefern Commerce Holdings is a mature public payments company, founded 2010, headquartered in Seattle WA, ~4,200 employees.

FINANCIALS (as of Q4 2025):
- TTM revenue: $1.8B (+11% YoY — growth slowing from 18% prior year)
- Net income TTM: $210M (profitable)
- Strong enterprise segment, legacy strength in in-person/POS

RECENT MOVES:
- Feb 2026: Announced acquisition of Graphlock Terminal Systems for $340M — consolidation play in retail POS
- Jan 2026: CFO departed; interim CFO in place while search continues
- Dec 2025: Disappointing Q3 results, stock down ~15% on slowing growth

COMPETITIVE: Market leader in POS segment. Losing share to nimbler players in online-first payments. Acquisition-heavy strategy over last 18 months.

title: Ironvane Freight Systems — Company Profile
body:
FICTIONAL COMPANY.

Ironvane Freight Systems is a supply chain software company, founded 2019, headquartered in Denver CO, ~620 employees.

FINANCIALS (as of Q4 2025):
- TTM revenue: $78M (+41% YoY)
- High burn — last reported $8M/month
- Series C, $95M raised Oct 2024

RECENT MOVES:
- Jan 2026: CPO Dana Okafor departed (subsequently joined Tinplate Merchant Systems as Head of Platform)
- Dec 2025: Landed major contract with a top-5 US retailer — largest deal in company history
- Nov 2025: Quiet layoff, ~8% of headcount

COMPETITIVE: Strong in warehouse/inventory management. Building out transportation optimization. Smaller footprint than incumbents but growing fast in mid-market.

title: Executive Briefing Format Guidelines
body:
Standard format for pre-meeting company briefings:

1. LEAD WITH THE HOOK — what's the one thing the exec needs to know? Recent news, strategic shift, risk signal.
2. NUMBERS — revenue, growth rate, profitability, cash position. Keep it to 3-4 data points.
3. RECENT MOVES — product launches, hires, incidents. Last 90 days.
4. COMPETITIVE CONTEXT — who are they up against, how are they positioned.
5. TALKING POINTS — 2-3 things worth bringing up in the meeting.

Keep it under 200 words. Bullets over paragraphs. Specific over vague — 'revenue grew 34%' beats 'revenue grew significantly.'
